MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-26-2023 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PETITIONER'S MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. ARBITRATION AWARD IS CONFIRMED; REQUEST FOR PRE-JUDGMENT INTEREST IS GRANTED; COSTS GRANTED AS REQUESTED; ATTORNEY'S FEES REASONABLY INCURRED IN BRINGING THIS PETITION GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,200 (4 HOURS AT $300/HOUR). OPPOSING PARTY FAILS TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THIS PETITIONER HAD TO BRING ITS REQUEST TO CONFIRM THE ARBITRATION AWARD AS TO THEM IN THE CASE BROUGHT BY ANOTHER PARTY EXPRESSLY LIMITED TO THE CONFIRMATION OF THAT PARTY'S AWARD. THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF ALL FILINGS IN CASE CGC-22-517913, WHICH IS EXTENSIVELY DISCUSSED BY BOTH SIDES IN THIS ACTION. OPPOSING PARTY ALSO FAILS TO SHOW THAT RES JUDICATA APPLIES IN THIS ACTION. THE PURPORTEDLY INCONSISTENT POSITION TAKEN BY THE PETITIONER APPEARS IN MARCH 22, 2023 FILING IN ACTION 517913. WHILE THAT POSITION WAS TAKEN PRIOR TO THE COURT'S RULING ON RESPONDENT'S SECOND PETITION TO VACATE THE ARBITRATION AWARD, THE COURT DID NOT ADOPT THAT POSITION AS TRUE. THE COURT'S RULING ON THE SECOND MOTION TO VACATE THE ARBITRATION AWARD IN ACTION CGC-22-514913 WAS BASED ON TWO GROUNDS: (1) BY MARCH 24, 2023, WHEN PETITION TO VACATE WAS HEARD, THE ACTION WAS OVER AND REDUCED TO JUDGMENT; (2) THE COURT HAS PREVIOUSLY RULED ON A PETITION TO VACATE, MAKING THE SECOND PETITION IMPROPER. NEITHER OF THESE GROUNDS ARE THE GROUNDS UPON WHICH RESPONDENT'S JUDICIAL ESTOPPEL ARGUMENT IS BASED. FURTHERMORE, OPPOSING PARTY FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE ISSUE OF CONFIRMATION/VACATUR OF THIS PETITIONER'S ARBITRATION AWARD HAS ALREADY BEEN ADVERSELY DETERMINED AGAINST THE PETITIONER. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

calendar.matters:
On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arb Award And For Entry Of Judgmnt
Off Calendar Jul-26-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER'S MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. Arbitration award is confirmed; request for pre-judgment interest is granted; costs granted as requested; attorney's fees reasonably incurred in bringing this petition granted in the amount of $1,200 (4 hours at $300/hour). Opposing party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that this petitioner had to bring its request to confirm the arbitration award as to them in the case brought by another party expressly limited to the confirmation of that party's award. The Court takes judicial notice of all filings in case CGC-22-517913, which is extensively discussed by both sides in this action. Opposing party also fails to show that res judicata applies in this action. The purportedly inconsistent position taken by the petitioner appears in March 22, 2023 filing in action 517913. While that position was taken prior to the Court's ruling on respondent's second petition to vacate the arbitration award, the Court did not adopt that position as true. The Court's ruling on the second motion to vacate the arbitration award in action CGC-22-514913 was based on two grounds: (1) by March 24, 2023, when petition to vacate was heard, the action was over and reduced to judgment; (2) the Court has previously ruled on a petition to vacate, making the second petition improper. Neither of these grounds are the grounds upon which respondent's judicial estoppel argument is based. Furthermore, opposing party fails to show that the issue of confirmation/vacatur of this petitioner's arbitration award has already been adversely determined against the petitioner. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)
